skill.name: defense-in-depth
skill.description: Guide AI coding agents to add validation at entry, business, environment, and diagnostic layers.
skill.summary: Build layered validation so invalid data fails early and predictably.
skill.author: DYAI2025

security_audit.summary: The external command and environment findings are false positives from markdown examples and inline text. No executable scripts, network calls, secret access, prompt injection, or malicious intent were found. One low-risk content concern remains because the skill recommends debug logging of working directories and stack traces.

low_findings.title: Debug Logging May Expose Local Paths
low_findings.description: The skill recommends logging directory, current working directory, and stack trace values for diagnostics. This is useful for debugging but can expose local paths or call context if logs are shared broadly.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited example explicitly includes directory, process.cwd(), and stack values in debug logs. The risk is low because this is documentation, not executable code.

content.value_statement: Invalid data often fails far from where it enters a system. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code add validation across multiple layers.
actual_capabilities: Explains four validation layers for application data flow.
Guides entry point checks for empty or invalid paths.
Shows business logic validation before sensitive operations.
Describes environment guards for safer test execution.
Recommends diagnostic logging to trace validation bypasses.
limitations: Does not provide runnable scripts or reusable library code.
Examples use TypeScript and may need adaptation for other languages.
Does not replace threat modeling or code review.
Debug logging guidance needs privacy review before production use.

use_cases.title: Harden Input Boundaries
use_cases.description: Add validation where user or test data first enters an application.

use_cases.title: Prevent Test Environment Damage
use_cases.description: Add guards that stop risky operations from running outside expected test directories.

use_cases.title: Review Validation Gaps
use_cases.description: Map data flow and identify layers where invalid values can bypass checks.

prompt_templates.title: Find Validation Layers
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the defense-in-depth skill to review this bug. Identify entry, business, environment, and diagnostic validation layers.

prompt_templates.title: Add Missing Checks
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the defense-in-depth skill to add validation at every layer this value passes through. Keep the change focused.

prompt_templates.title: Test Bypass Paths
prompt_templates.scenario: Validate that checks cannot be skipped
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the defense-in-depth skill to design tests that try to bypass each validation layer.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Data Flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the defense-in-depth skill to trace this data flow across modules and propose layered validation with residual risks.

output_examples.input: A project directory can be empty during test setup.
output_examples.output: The response maps the directory value from setup to workspace creation and proposes checks at entry, business, and test guard layers.

output_examples.input: A dangerous operation runs from the wrong working directory.
output_examples.output: The response recommends environment-specific guards and diagnostic logging, with a note to avoid exposing sensitive paths.

output_examples.input: A single validation check was added but the bug returned.
output_examples.output: The response identifies bypass paths and suggests additional validation where mocks or alternate callers skip the first check.

best_practices: Trace the full data flow before adding checks.
Validate close to every important operation, not only at the first input.
Review diagnostic logs for sensitive path or context exposure.
anti_patterns: Relying on one boundary check for all callers.
Adding debug logs that expose sensitive data without review.
Treating tests and mocks as trusted validation layers.

faq.question: What problem does this skill solve?
faq.answer: It helps prevent invalid data from reaching sensitive operations through alternate code paths.

faq.question: Does it run commands?
faq.answer: No. The skill is documentation with TypeScript examples and does not include executable scripts.

faq.question: Why use multiple validation layers?
faq.answer: Different callers, mocks, and environments can bypass one check. Multiple layers reduce that risk.

faq.question: Can I use it outside TypeScript?
faq.answer: Yes. The pattern applies broadly, but examples need translation to the target language.

faq.question: Is the debug logging advice safe?
faq.answer: It can be safe for local debugging, but review logs for paths, stack traces, and sensitive context.

faq.question: Is this a replacement for security review?
faq.answer: No. It supports validation design but does not replace threat modeling or manual review.
